paper_title: GDGT Analysis and Age-Model Construction in Lake Chala Sediments

methods.description: Process for analyzing GDGTs in sediment samples to infer climate proxies.

steps.description: 396 sediment horizons of 2-cm thickness and sampled at regular 16-cm intervals were selected for GDGT analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: select sediment horizons for GDGT analysis

steps.description: Freeze-dried and powdered sediments (0.3-1 g dry weight) were extracted using a Dionex accelerated solvent extraction system with a 9:1 (v/v) mixture of dichloromethane (DCM) and methanol.
steps.searchable_summary: extract lipids from sediment using solvent extraction

steps.description: An internal standard (1 µg of synthetic C46 glycerol trialkyl glycerol tetraether) was added to the total lipid extract.
steps.searchable_summary: add internal standard to lipid extract

steps.description: The total lipid extracts were dissolved in DCM, passed through a sodium sulfate column and dried under nitrogen gas.
steps.searchable_summary: purify lipid extract using sodium sulfate column

steps.description: Subjected to chromatography using an Al2O3 column with eluents of hexane/DCM (9:1, v/v), hexane/DCM (1:1, v/v) and DCM/methanol (1:1, v/v) to obtain apolar, ketone and polar fractions, respectively, which were dried under nitrogen gas.
The GDGT-containing polar fractions were redissolved in hexane/isopropanol (99:1, v/v) and filtered using a polytetrafluoroethylene 0.45-µm filter before analysis.
Analysis using an Agilent 1260 Infinity UHPLC system coupled to an Agilent 6130 mass spectrometer.
GDGTs were identified by detecting the [M + H]+ ions in selected ion monitoring mode for the relevant m/z values.
Peak area integration was done using Agilent Masshunter software and a peak area of 3 × 10^3 units was applied as the detection threshold.
steps.searchable_summary: integrate peak areas using Masshunter software

steps.description: The fractional abundances of the individual brGDGTs were calculated relative to the sum of all 15 brGDGTs.

The variability in the fractional abundance of brGDGTs in the sediment record was compared with that in suspended particulate matter (SPM) from the Lake Chala water column using principal component analysis in the R statistical package FactoMineR.
steps.searchable_summary: perform PCA on brGDGTs using FactoMineR

methods.description: Construction and validation of an age-depth model for the DeepCHALLA sediment sequence using radiometric dating and sediment accumulation rates.

steps.description: The age-depth model of the studied DeepCHALLA sediment sequence is based on partial varve counting and high-resolution radiometric (210Pb and 14C) dating.
steps.searchable_summary: construct age-depth model using varve counting and radiometric dating

steps.description: Morphological and geochemical identification of 73.7-kyr old YTT cryptotephra recovered at 62.36 mefd.
steps.searchable_summary: identify YTT cryptotephra for age model

steps.description: Constraints on long-term variation in the rate of sediment accumulation associated with depositional continuity and uniform lithology.
steps.searchable_summary: apply constraints on sediment accumulation rates

steps.description: Extrapolation of sedimentation rates to estimate ages beyond the 14C-dated portion of the sequence.
steps.searchable_summary: extrapolate sedimentation rates for age estimation

steps.description: Incorporate all absolute age markers into a Bayesian age model.
steps.searchable_summary: create Bayesian age model with absolute age markers

steps.description: Produce an age-depth curve with 2σ uncertainty ranges.
